articles.summary: With Marqeta, launch payments experiences without choosing between innovation and scale. Marqeta combines the scale and reliability of proven payments infrastructure with the flexibility and innovation of a modern platform [4]—so you can move faster, reduce risk, and grow with confidence.

articles.title: TOWARD A WORKING DEFINITION OF PAPERCLIP-PUNK
articles.summary: Paperclip-punk, a new AI-era aesthetic, is marked by clean, interactive, blueprint-like web design that reflects growing machine agency. It represents a shift toward interfaces that teach and respond. While defining it gives the movement clarity, it may also make it easier for AI to replicate and absorb.

articles.title: AI, NETWORKS, AND MECHANICAL TURKS
articles.summary: LLMs let companies borrow intent-level understanding instead of building it, changing how recommendation and discovery systems start, scale, and shape what people see.

articles.title: STOP TALKING ABOUT YOUR IMPACT. START SPOTLIGHTING THEIRS
articles.summary: Recognizing others is the most authentic form of self-advocacy. You elevate everyone, including yourself, when you share specific wins, link them to strategy, and run internal launches that spotlight team contributions.

articles.title: WHY GREAT EXECUTIVES MUST STILL DO THE WORK
articles.summary: Leaders who stop doing the work lose the sharpness that made them leaders in the first place. In the AI era, staying hands-on is a competitive advantage.

articles.title: HOW TO CREATE A GREAT DIGITAL ONBOARDING EXPERIENCE, EVEN IN HIGHLY REGULATED INDUSTRIES
articles.summary: Frictionless signup is not always simple - but making your onboarding more user-friendly is always going to be a competitive advantage. This guide by Persona [10] explains how you can use passive, risk-based signals to determine which types of verifications to employ, reduce onboarding friction, and increase conversion rates. articles.title: THE STATE OF AI
articles.summary: LLMs are used across many tasks, with open models gaining ground and programming and roleplay leading demand. Capabilities, not price, drive adoption. Breakthrough models often form uniquely loyal early user groups.

articles.title: HOW TO ASK FOR SUPPORT FROM SENIOR LEADERS
articles.summary: Senior leaders' offers to help are genuine, and asking for support is a core leadership skill. Knowing when to ask and what type of help you need can speed up decisions, strengthen alignment, and raise your profile.

articles.title: SO WHAT'S GOING TO HAPPEN TO PRODUCT MANAGEMENT ANYWAY?
articles.summary: AI-native companies need fewer PMs, but the PMs who stay will be closer to the product, move faster, and operate with stronger technical and alignment skills. The winners will be builders who ship quickly, guide teams through ambiguity, and adapt to a new pace of work.

articles.title: WHY LOCAL TECH SCENES HAVE CHANGED
articles.summary: AI reshuffled local tech. Top talent either move to SF or build solo, leaving local scenes as communities of independent creators rather than traditional startup pipelines.

articles.title: AN OUTBOUND PLAYBOOK FOR 2025
articles.summary: Modern outbound wins by combining AI-scored ICPs with layered signals and human-personalized outreach, turning content engagement into the engine of a unified GTM flywheel.

articles.title: FAILURE AS A FEATURE, NOT A BUG
articles.summary: Growth depends on risk-taking, and teams thrive when leaders normalize failure and create psychological safety.
